title: Bucketize
description:
Given values $v\in\mathbb{R}^n$ and a set of boundary thresholds $\text{boundaries}\in\mathbb{R}^m$, return for each $v[k]$ the count of boundaries strictly less than $v[k]$ (its bucket index).

**Shapes**: `v`:[n], `boundaries`:[m] -> output:[n].
starter_code:
import torch

def bucketize(v, boundaries):
    # v: [n], boundaries: [m]; output[k] = count of boundaries < v[k]
    # TODO
    pass
hints: Build an `[n, m]` boolean matrix comparing every `boundaries[t]` against every `v[k]`.
Matmuling that boolean matrix with a length-m vector of ones sums each row into the bucket count, avoiding `sum`/`cumsum` — 1 line, broadcasting only.

solutions.name: Broadcast comparison + matmul sum
solutions.explanation: `boundaries[None, :] < v[:, None]` broadcasts to an `[n, m]` boolean matrix that is $1$ at $(k,t)$ exactly when $\text{boundaries}_t < v_k$. Matmuling with a vector of ones sums each row, giving the count of boundaries below each $v_k$ — its bucket index — with no explicit sort or loop required.
